title: severinia monophylla
intro: Severinia monophylla is a plant species within the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the Chinese box orange or Chinese box myrtle. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's bark and leaves are used to prepare herbal remedies, which have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of colds and flu, and soothing digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Severinia monophylla include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
